spec.acceptance_rule: At 1440, 1280, 1024, 820, 720, and 390 px, the watchlist regions do not overlap and documentElement.scrollWidth <= innerWidth.

commands.note: repo-local install of playwright-core 1.60.0 / lightweight-charts 5.2.0; no global install
rebuild outputs/dashboard-preview (index.html, app.js, dashboard-core.js, styles.css, lightweight-charts.js, tqr-logo.svg)
headless Chromium 148.0.7778.96 via playwright-core; also starts scripts/tqe_sidecar.py on 127.0.0.1 and serves outputs/dashboard-preview on 127.0.0.1

acceptance_notes: All six TQR-WIREFRAME-002 breakpoints (1440/1280/1024/820/720/390 px) pass: documentElement.scrollWidth equals innerWidth at every breakpoint, so the application document has no horizontal scrollbar.

The four watchlist command regions (group management, symbol search plus primary add action, clear/save actions, persistence status) do not overlap at any breakpoint; the smoke asserts pairwise bounding-box non-overlap at each size.
Browser smoke reports no page errors and no non-loopback/external requests; the visual baseline (8 deterministic screenshot hashes) also matches.
Table overflow stays inside .table-responsive wrappers; the overflow check is against the document root, matching the spec acceptance criterion.
Evidence produced on the current working tree, which includes the staged modification to scripts/dashboard-browser-smoke.cjs; no git mutations were performed.
